formations_blocks: Iran high pressing and counter-pressing early
New Zealand settling into two defensive lines after scoring
adjustments: New Zealand increasingly using long balls and direct play to Chris Wood after the goal
Iran's early high-energy press loses momentum after conceding
key_matchups: Chris Wood vs multiple Iran defenders
Marko Stamenić vs Iran's penalty-area defenders on second balls
phases.summary: Iran began with intense pressure, quickly winning the ball back and repeatedly attacking down the right, but New Zealand matched the physical start and then struck first through direct play. After the goal, New Zealand dropped into two compact defensive lines and looked to launch Chris Wood, whose hold-up play and aerial presence repeatedly troubled an increasingly rattled Iran back line.

formations_blocks: New Zealand all-out attack / high attacking block
end-to-end open game with both sides attacking quickly
key_matchups: Mehdi Taremi vs the New Zealand defence
phases.summary: This phase is defined by New Zealand’s aggressive, all-out attacking approach, which repeatedly finds Chris Wood in space, while Iran initially survive and then start to exploit the openings on the counter. After 20 minutes Iran gain more control in midfield and begin sustaining pressure, with Taremi nearly scoring before Rezaeian’s equaliser arrives in a chaotic, open contest.

formations_blocks: New Zealand happy to build with early crosses from deeper areas
Iran allowing New Zealand extra time on the ball in their own half
key_matchups: New Zealand crosses targeting Chris Wood
phases.summary: The first half was played at a brisk, open tempo, with both sides committed to attacking rather than settling into a cautious block. New Zealand were especially direct, using early delivery toward Chris Wood, while Iran were content to let them have extra time in their own half at 42 minutes as the game moved toward half-time. The overall pattern was balanced and chaotic, reflected in the near-even possession and the shot count slightly favoring New Zealand.

formations_blocks: New Zealand attacking through direct balls into Chris Wood's feet
Iran sustained attacking pressure after conceding, including corners and goalmouth scrambles
adjustments: 53': Iran substitute Ali Alipour for Shahriar Moghanloo, adding another attacker
New Zealand continue to build attacks around Wood dropping in and linking with runners
Iran respond immediately after going 1-2 down with quicker, more direct pressure in the box
key_matchups: Chris Wood vs Iran's centre-backs in aerial/direct buildup
Eli Just vs Iran's back line on the break
Max Crocombe vs Iran's close-range efforts during the scramble
phases.summary: The phase stayed open and transitional, with New Zealand repeatedly targeting Chris Wood as a focal point and Iran looking to counter quickly whenever possession changed. After Iran switched to a more attacking setup by introducing Ali Alipour, New Zealand struck through Eli Just on a direct combination with Wood, but Iran again answered with heavy box pressure and soon equalised through Mohebi.

formations_blocks: New Zealand low block/compact penalty-area defending
Iran on top in the attacking third with sustained pressure
adjustments: Tim Payne substituted for Callan Elliot to deal with Iran's pacey attackers
Mehdi Taremi replaced by Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh
New Zealand release pressure to launch counterattacks late on
New Zealand bench/shape changes in the final minutes while protecting the lead/pressure
key_matchups: Saeid Ezatolahi vs New Zealand box defending
phases.summary: The closing phase is dominated by Iran’s pressure in the final third, while New Zealand collapse into a compact penalty-area block to repel crosses and second balls. Substitutions on both sides reflect the game state: New Zealand add fresh legs at right back to cope with pace, Iran replace Taremi with Hosseinzadeh, and the hosts try to spring late counters when space appears.
